blocks.body: Present Perfect Continuous — have / has been + V-ing for actions that started in the past and continue now, duration, and recent activity with a visible result.

{"level":"A2–B1","intro":"Present Perfect Continuous shows a process or duration: the action started in the past, continued for some time, and is still happening now or has just finished with a visible result.","rules":[{"label":"Forms","color":"emerald","text":"Start with the model have / has been + V-ing.","example":"I have been working. / She has been waiting."},{"label":"Duration to now","color":"blue","text":"The tense is often used when an action started earlier and continues up to the present moment.","example":"We have been living here for two years. / He has been studying since morning."},{"label":"Visible result","color":"amber","text":"You can also use it for recent activity when we can see the result now.","example":"She has been running. / They have been cleaning the kitchen."},{"label":"Time expressions","color":"violet","text":"Words like for, since, how long, lately, recently, all day, this week often point to this tense.","example":"I have been working all day. / How long have you been waiting?"}]}

{"title":"1. Lessons in order","intro":"Start with the form, then move to negatives, questions, and time expressions.","items":[{"label":"A2–B1","title":"Forms and Use","subtitle":"have / has been + V-ing and the main meanings of the tense."},{"label":"A2–B1","title":"Negatives","subtitle":"haven't / hasn't been + V-ing for actions that are not continuing or not happening now."},{"label":"A2–B1","title":"Questions and Short Answers","subtitle":"Have you been...?, Has she been...?, How long have they been...?"},{"label":"A2–B1","title":"Time Expressions","subtitle":"for, since, how long, lately, recently, all day, all morning, this week."}]}

{"title":"2. Where you need this tense","sections":[{"label":"Action continuing now","color":"emerald","description":"Show an action that started in the past and is still continuing.","examples":[{"en":"I have been learning English for six months.","ua":"The activity continues now."},{"en":"She has been waiting since 9 o'clock.","ua":"The action started earlier and is still in progress."}]},{"label":"Duration up to now","color":"blue","description":"Focus not only on the fact, but also on how long the action has continued.","examples":[{"en":"We have been working all morning.","ua":"The duration matters."},{"en":"He has been driving for three hours.","ua":"The process has lasted for a period of time."}]},{"label":"Recent action with a result","color":"amber","description":"Talk about recent activity when the result is visible now.","examples":[{"en":"You are wet. Have you been walking in the rain?","ua":"The result is visible now."},{"en":"She is tired because she has been cleaning.","ua":"A recent process explains the present result."}]},{"label":"Focus on the process","color":"violet","description":"This tense often highlights the process and duration, not only the final result.","examples":[{"en":"I have been reading this book this week.","ua":"The week is not finished yet."},{"en":"They have been talking a lot lately.","ua":"Recent repeated activity."}]}]}

{"title":"3. In short","items":["Present Perfect Continuous = have / has been + V-ing.","This tense is used for actions that started in the past and continue up to now.","It often highlights duration, process, or recent activity with a visible result.","Words like for, since, how long, lately, and recently often point to this tense."]}
